# MSA Playbook Matrix

Reference table from the Trinetix Contract Playbook. Use these as the deterministic clause-by-clause ceiling alongside the per-type SKILL.md.

| ID / Topic | Preferred position | Acceptable fallback | Red flags | Escalation | AI instruction |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MSA-001 Structure and SOW precedence | Use MSA as framework. Services require SOW. SOW controls only for services under that SOW. | Accept SOW-specific deviations if limited to that SOW and approved by relevant owner. | SOW overrides entire MSA generally; oral/email changes; client policies incorporated by reference without review. | Escalate broad order-of-precedence changes. | Check if conflicts are limited to the relevant SOW. Flag any broad client-policy supremacy. |
| MSA-002 Change requests | No change effective unless written and signed, unless SOW says otherwise. | Operational tickets may guide work but should not amend legal scope unless tied to approved change process. | Client can unilaterally change requirements, deadlines, security obligations, or scope without price/time adjustment. | Escalate unilateral change language. | Identify whether change process protects timeline and price. |
| MSA-003 Consultant status | Consultants remain under Vendor relationship; no employment, leased workforce, agency, fiduciary, or joint-employer status. | Client may give day-to-day task priorities within SOW, but Vendor remains independent. | Client controls employment terms; client claims right to discipline, hire, fire, or supervise as employer. | Escalate employment/joint-employer risk. | Flag clauses inconsistent with independent contractor model. |
| MSA-004 Consultant replacement | Vendor may replace consultants with reasonable notice and equivalent qualifications; client may request replacement for cause or convenience with notice. | Shorter notice may be accepted for cause if operationally realistic. | Client veto over all replacements; named individuals guaranteed without substitution; penalties for lawful absence. | Escalate hard individual-retention commitments. | Check whether replacement process preserves continuity without creating staffing guarantee. |
| MSA-005 Service location and sanctions | Remote services permitted from Vendor-selected locations, excluding sanctioned/prohibited locations; client must state location restrictions before SOW. | Accept reasonable client country restrictions if known before staffing and commercially feasible. | Blanket prohibition on offshore/nearshore work; retroactive restrictions; undefined geopolitical restrictions. | Escalate delivery-location restrictions. | Flag if location limits affect delivery model, cost, or staffing. |
| MSA-006 Client responsibilities | Client must provide information, access, approvals, systems, licenses, and timely instructions. Vendor may suspend if client materially hinders performance. | Accept notice/cure period before suspension if payment and delivery risk are still protected. | Vendor liable for client-caused delays; no time/price adjustment for late client inputs. | Escalate if client cooperation protections are removed. | Identify dependencies and whether delay relief is preserved. |
| MSA-007 Warranties | Services performed professionally by qualified consultants and deliverables conform to SOW specs. Other warranties disclaimed. | Accept reasonable professional-services warranty tied to SOW and cure/re-performance remedy. | Fitness for purpose, uninterrupted/error-free software, strict compliance with all client policies, broad non-infringement warranty not tied to Vendor materials. | Escalate broad warranties. | Separate service-quality warranty from outcome guarantee. |
| MSA-008 Security | Vendor maintains agreed security measures; detailed obligations should be in SOW, security appendix, or separate agreement. | Accept reasonable information requests and client security policy if reviewed before SOW. | Unlimited audit rights; access to Vendor systems; immediate incident notice for any event; compliance with unknown future policies. | Escalate security addenda and audit rights. | Check if obligations are concrete, feasible, costed, and limited to services. |
| MSA-009 Privacy and DPA | Parties comply with privacy laws. If Vendor processes client PII, use separate DPA. | Accept client DPA after privacy review. | PII terms inside MSA that impose controller obligations on Vendor when Vendor is processor; unclear data roles; broad sensitive data processing. | Privacy counsel review required. | Flag whether DPA is needed and whether personal data categories are described. |
| MSA-010 Sanctions and export controls | Each party complies with applicable trade laws. Client cannot require services or deliverables that expose Vendor to sanctions risk. Vendor may refuse/suspend affected performance. | Accept mutual compliance wording if Vendor suspension/refusal right remains. | Client requires support to sanctioned territories/persons; no right to suspend; client makes Vendor responsible for client export decisions. | Escalate to CLCO. | Check if Vendor can lawfully withhold affected services without liability. |
| MSA-011 Payment term | Default payment within 14 calendar days from invoice date; no set-off without consent; USD; client bears conversion costs. | Fallback: 30 days for strategic clients if Finance approves. | Net 45/60/90; pay-when-paid; broad set-off; payment only after acceptance where acceptance is vague. | Finance approval for >30 days or set-off. | Extract payment period, currency, set-off, invoice dispute period, tax gross-up. |
| MSA-012 Invoice disputes | Client must dispute invoice within 5 business days; undisputed amounts remain payable. | Accept 10 business days if Finance approves. | No deemed approval; withholding all invoice amounts due to partial dispute. | Finance approval required. | Check if undisputed amounts must be paid on time. |
| MSA-013 Out-of-scope work | Client-requested work outside SOW is compensable at latest relevant rates or standard rates. | Accept change-order requirement if urgent work protection remains. | Out-of-scope work deemed included; no compensation absent formal amendment despite client request. | Escalate material fixed-price scope risk. | Flag any clause making extra work free or unbillable. |
| MSA-014 Taxes and gross-up | Prices are net. Client bears indirect taxes and withholding gross-up except taxes on Vendor income. | Accept local tax wording after tax review. | Client may withhold without gross-up; Vendor bears customer jurisdiction taxes; reverse-charge not handled clearly. | Tax/Finance review required. | Identify tax withholding, VAT/sales tax, gross-up, exemption certificate obligations. |
| MSA-015 Late payment and suspension | Vendor may charge late fee, recover collection costs, suspend services, withhold deliverables, require prepayment, and terminate for non-payment. IP transfer is suspended until full payment. | Accept notice before suspension if short and preserves leverage. | No suspension right; continued service despite non-payment; IP transfer despite non-payment. | Escalate removal of suspension/IP payment protection. | Flag any weakening of payment remedies. |
| MSA-016 Insurance | Maintain CGL, workers comp/employer liability, umbrella/excess, and Technology E&O with privacy, cyber, media, IP, personal injury coverage. | Client additional insured on request if available by endorsement. | Coverage amounts above current limits, broad waiver of subrogation, insurer notice obligations not supported by policy. | Finance/Risk review. | Extract requested coverages and compare to standard. |
| MSA-017 IP assignment | Client receives deliverable IP only after full payment for relevant services. Works-made-for-hire plus assignment. Background IP, FOSS, and Vendor-licensed IP excluded. | Accept assignment on creation only if license/ownership is expressly suspended until payment and Background IP remains protected. | Assignment before payment; assignment of Background IP; client ownership of tools, templates, methods, know-how, libraries, or general skills. | CLCO approval required. | Identify when IP transfers and what is excluded. |
| MSA-018 Background IP and know-how | Vendor retains Background IP and reusable know-how. Client receives limited license to Background IP embedded in deliverables after payment. | Accept broader use right only as needed to use deliverables. | Client may use Background IP separately, sublicense it broadly, or restrict Vendor reuse of general knowledge. | Escalate to CLCO. | Flag ownership/reuse restrictions that limit future business. |
| MSA-019 FOSS and third-party IP | Vendor incorporates FOSS or Vendor-Licensed IP only if client instructs; identify components where reasonable. | Accept Vendor-selected FOSS if delivery approves and license process exists. | Strict warranty that no FOSS exists; indemnity for client-mandated FOSS; ban on all open source without operational review. | Delivery/legal review. | Check whether FOSS risk follows instruction/control. |
| MSA-020 GenAI | Vendor may use GenAI responsibly. Client confidential information cannot be used as input/training data except as needed for services and only with secure non-training processing. | Accept client notice/disclosure obligation for tools used. | Blanket ban on AI that blocks standard development tools; client ownership uncertainty; requirement to disclose prompts or internal methods; unlimited liability for AI output. | CLCO/Security review. | Identify AI use restrictions, training-data rules, disclosure obligations, and IP warranty issues. |
| MSA-021 Confidentiality | Mutual confidentiality, need-to-know disclosure to personnel/affiliates/subcontractors, reasonable care, 3-year survival, trade secrets indefinite. | Accept 5-year survival if operationally feasible. | Perpetual protection for all information; no affiliates/subcontractors; no legal/compliance retention copy; no professional-advisor disclosure. | Escalate operationally blocking restrictions. | Check survival period, permitted recipients, retention, and trade-secret treatment. |
| MSA-022 Marketing / case studies | Parties may use name/logo in counterparty lists; Vendor may use limited deliverable examples without proprietary info; public content subject to client approval not unreasonably withheld. | Accept prior written approval for logo/case studies. | Total ban on reference use for strategic client may be commercial decision. | Sales/Executive approval. | Flag restrictions on portfolio/case-study rights. |
| MSA-023 Non-solicitation | Mutual restriction on soliciting restricted persons, referrals, and circumvention; 2-year survival. | Accept shorter term or narrowed group if business approves. | Broad non-compete; restriction on hiring anyone who applies independently; liquidated damages too high; applies beyond reasonable group/territory. | CLCO approval. | Distinguish non-solicit from non-compete and flag enforceability/business issues. |
| MSA-024 Indemnity | Mutual third-party indemnity for breach, gross negligence/willful misconduct/wrongful acts, and IP infringement from party materials/technology, with exclusions. | Accept client-specific IP indemnity if limited to Vendor-created deliverables and excludes client materials, instructions, modifications, combinations, and FOSS/client tools. | First-party indemnity; uncapped broad indemnity; indemnity for client instructions, data, third-party systems, or regulatory fines not caused by Vendor. | CLCO approval. | Classify whether indemnity is third-party, capped, and causally tied to Vendor control. |
| MSA-025 Liability cap | Exclude indirect/special/consequential/punitive damages. Aggregate cap equals amounts paid/payable in prior 12 months, with special formula for first 12 months. | Fallback: higher cap or SOW-specific cap with CLCO approval and pricing rationale. | Unlimited liability; cap based on all affiliates/all SOWs; carve-outs for ordinary negligence, payment disputes, data breach without cap, service credits uncapped. | CLCO approval. | Extract cap amount, basis, period, carve-outs, and whether payment obligations are excluded. |
| MSA-026 Termination | MSA indefinite. Either party may terminate MSA/SOW for convenience on 60 days notice. Immediate termination for non-payment, uncured material breach, long suspension, insolvency, other causes. | Accept longer convenience notice if resource ramp-down is protected. | Client may terminate immediately for convenience without paying committed fees, ramp-down, or work performed; termination voids payment/IP protections. | Finance/Delivery/CLCO review. | Check termination rights and payment consequences. |
| MSA-027 Governing law / disputes | Tennessee and U.S. law; disputes in Nashville, Tennessee, AAA arbitration with single arbitrator. | Accept Delaware/New York/English law only after CLCO review. | Unfamiliar courts, local language proceedings, mandatory client home venue, injunctive relief only for client. | CLCO approval. | Flag law/forum/arbitration changes. |
| MSA-028 Assignment / notices / publicity / counterparts | Keep standard operational clauses, electronic signatures, notice details, assignment limits, severability, entire agreement. | Accept reasonable assignment to affiliates/successors. | Client may assign to competitor or distressed entity without consent; notices by portal only; client policies update automatically. | Legal review. | Check operational enforceability and notice channels. |
